title: Cleaning Cycle Required (Backflush)
shortDescription: The machine needs a cleaning backflush cycle to remove coffee oils from the brew group.
fullDescription: The CLEAN ME alert indicates the machine needs a backflush cleaning cycle. Coffee oils accumulate in the group head, shower screen, and internal pathways. The cleaning cycle uses a cleaning tablet dissolved in water to flush these oils out through the three-way solenoid valve. Neglecting this maintenance leads to bitter-tasting coffee, reduced flow rate, and eventual blockages.

causes.cause: Normal coffee oil accumulation from regular use
Usage counter reached cleaning threshold (approximately 200 shots)
Previous cleaning cycle not completed fully
Using very oily dark roast beans that deposit residue faster

symptoms: CLEAN ME message on LCD display or dedicated LED
Espresso tastes bitter or rancid
Coffee oils visible around group head gasket
Reduced flow during extraction
Puck is soupy or wet after extraction

fixSteps.title: Insert the cleaning disc
fixSteps.instruction: Place the rubber cleaning disc (or single-wall filter basket) into the portafilter. Add one Breville cleaning tablet on top of the disc.

fixSteps.title: Start the cleaning cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Lock the portafilter in place. For Barista Express: press and hold the 1-cup and 2-cup buttons simultaneously. For models with LCD: navigate to Clean in the menu.

fixSteps.title: Let the cycle complete
fixSteps.instruction: The machine will run water through the group head, pause, and repeat several times. This dissolves coffee oils and flushes them through the three-way valve. The cycle takes about 5 minutes.

fixSteps.title: Clean the shower screen
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the portafilter and rinse it. Use a small brush to scrub the shower screen above the group head. For deep cleaning, unscrew the shower screen with a flathead screwdriver and soak it in hot water for 15 minutes.

whenToCallPro: Not needed. Backflushing is always a DIY maintenance task.
videoSearchQuery: Breville espresso CLEAN ME backflush how to fix

faq.q: What is backflushing?
faq.a: Backflushing pushes water and cleaning solution backward through the group head to flush out accumulated coffee oils from the three-way solenoid valve.

faq.q: How often should I backflush?
faq.a: Every 1-2 weeks with regular use. The CLEAN ME alert typically triggers every 200 shots.

faq.q: Can I skip the cleaning tablet?
faq.a: Running a water-only backflush is better than nothing, but the tablet is needed to dissolve coffee oils effectively.

title: Descaling Required
shortDescription: The machine requires descaling to remove mineral buildup from the thermocoil and boiler.
fullDescription: The DESCALE indicator activates when the internal usage counter determines that enough water has been processed to warrant descaling. Mineral deposits from hard water accumulate in the thermocoil, boiler, and internal tubing, reducing heating efficiency, restricting water flow, and affecting espresso taste and temperature.

causes.cause: Normal mineral buildup from regular use
Hard water accelerating scale formation
Previous descaling cycle not completed
Water filter not installed or expired

symptoms: DESCALE message on LCD or dedicated LED
Espresso extraction slowing down noticeably
Brew temperature lower than normal
Steam pressure reduced and weak
Pump sounds strained during operation

fixSteps.title: Prepare the solution
fixSteps.instruction: Dissolve one packet of Breville descaling powder in the water tank filled to the DESCALE line. Or use a mixture of 1 part white vinegar to 1 part water as an alternative.
tools: Breville descaling powder or white vinegar

fixSteps.title: Enter descaling mode
fixSteps.instruction: On Barista Express/Pro: Navigate to Settings > Descale on the display. On Bambino: Hold the 1-cup and 2-cup buttons for 3 seconds. Place a large container under both the coffee spout and steam wand.

fixSteps.title: Run the descaling cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Follow the on-screen prompts. The machine will flush descaling solution through the brew head and steam wand, alternating between pumping and pausing. This takes about 25 minutes.

fixSteps.title: Rinse cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Empty the tank, fill with fresh water, and run the rinse cycle. Repeat until no vinegar or solution taste remains — at least 2 full rinse cycles.

whenToCallPro: Only if the descale light does not clear after completing the full cycle.
videoSearchQuery: Breville espresso machine descale how to fix

faq.q: How often should I descale my Breville?
faq.a: Every 2-3 months, or when the DESCALE indicator activates. More often with hard water.

faq.q: Can I use citric acid to descale?
faq.a: Yes, citric acid (25g per liter) is an effective and gentle alternative to Breville descaling powder.

faq.q: Will descaling void my warranty?
faq.a: No, descaling is recommended maintenance. Not descaling and causing damage may void the warranty.

title: Drip Tray Full
shortDescription: The drip tray is full and needs to be emptied to continue brewing.
fullDescription: The EMPTY ME alert indicates the drip tray has collected enough liquid to require emptying. A float sensor in the drip tray triggers this alert. The machine will lock out brewing until the tray is emptied to prevent overflow and water damage.

causes.cause: Drip tray filled to capacity from normal use and purging
Float sensor stuck in raised position due to residue
Drip tray not properly seated after last emptying
Excessive purging or cleaning cycles filling tray quickly

symptoms: EMPTY ME message on display or indicator light on
Machine will not brew until tray is emptied
Water visible at tray level indicator
Water pooling around base of machine
Drip tray feels heavy when removed

fixSteps.title: Remove and empty the drip tray
fixSteps.instruction: Carefully slide the drip tray out from the front of the machine. Pour out the collected water and coffee liquid into the sink.

fixSteps.title: Clean the drip tray and grounds bin
fixSteps.instruction: Wash the drip tray with warm soapy water. If it has a grounds bin, empty that too. Rinse thoroughly and dry.

fixSteps.title: Check the float sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Look for the small float mechanism in the drip tray or its housing on the machine. Ensure it moves freely up and down. Clean any coffee residue preventing smooth movement.

fixSteps.title: Reseat the drip tray
fixSteps.instruction: Slide the drip tray firmly back into position. Ensure it is fully inserted and sits level. The alert should clear within a few seconds.

whenToCallPro: This never requires professional service unless the float sensor is broken and cannot be cleaned.
videoSearchQuery: Breville espresso machine EMPTY ME drip tray fix

faq.q: What does EMPTY ME mean on my Breville?
faq.a: EMPTY ME means the drip tray is full and needs to be emptied before you can brew again.

faq.q: Can I still brew with EMPTY ME showing?
faq.a: No, most Breville models lock out brewing until the tray is emptied to prevent overflow.

faq.q: How often should I empty the drip tray?
faq.a: Empty it whenever the alert appears, or daily for heavy users, to prevent overflow and mold growth.

title: Water Filter Needs Replacement
shortDescription: The internal water filter has reached its usage limit and needs replacement.
fullDescription: The REPLACE FILTER indicator activates when the charcoal water filter in the reservoir has been in use beyond its recommended lifespan (approximately 2-3 months or 60 liters). The filter reduces chlorine, sediment, and scale-causing minerals to improve taste and protect internal components.

causes.cause: Filter has been in use for 2+ months
Filter has processed the maximum water volume
Hard water saturating the filter faster than normal
Filter counter not reset after previous replacement

symptoms: REPLACE FILTER message on display
Coffee taste has changed or become flat
Scale buildup occurring faster than normal
Water flow through the filter is slow
Filter indicator light illuminated

fixSteps.title: Soak the new filter
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the new Breville water filter from its packaging. Soak it in fresh water for 5 minutes to activate the charcoal media.

fixSteps.title: Install the new filter
fixSteps.instruction: Open the filter holder in the water tank. Remove the old filter and insert the new one. Close the holder firmly.

fixSteps.title: Reset the filter counter
fixSteps.instruction: On the machine, navigate to Settings > Filter and select Reset. On simpler models, the counter resets automatically when the filter is replaced and reinserted.

fixSteps.title: Flush the new filter
fixSteps.instruction: Run 2-3 water-only shots through the machine to flush any loose charcoal particles from the new filter.

whenToCallPro: Not needed. Filter replacement is always a DIY task.
videoSearchQuery: Breville water filter replacement reset how to

faq.q: How often should I replace the Breville water filter?
faq.a: Every 2-3 months or after approximately 60 liters of water.

faq.q: Can I use my Breville without a water filter?
faq.a: Yes, but you will need to descale more frequently and water quality may affect coffee taste.

faq.q: Do I need to soak the new filter before use?
faq.a: Yes, soaking for 5 minutes activates the charcoal and removes loose particles.

title: Water Tank Empty or Not Detected
shortDescription: The machine cannot detect the water tank or the tank is empty.
fullDescription: Error E01 indicates the machine attempted to draw water but the water tank is empty, not properly seated, or the tank sensor has failed. The machine will not operate any water function until the tank is detected with adequate water.

causes.cause: Water tank is empty
Water tank not properly seated on the valve mechanism
Water tank valve or sensor blocked by coffee grounds or debris
Faulty water tank detection sensor or reed switch

symptoms: E01 error on display
Water tank light flashing
Pump makes noise but no water flows
Machine stops mid-brew unexpectedly
Steam wand produces no steam

fixSteps.title: Fill and reseat the water tank
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the water tank. Fill it with fresh cold water to the maximum line. Firmly press it back into position, ensuring the valve at the bottom seats properly onto the machine's water inlet.

fixSteps.title: Clean the tank valve
fixSteps.instruction: Check the rubber valve at the bottom of the tank and the machine's water inlet for coffee grounds or mineral buildup. Clean both with a damp cloth.

fixSteps.title: Check the water filter
fixSteps.instruction: If using a water filter, ensure it is not clogged and blocking water flow. Remove the filter temporarily to test if E01 clears.

fixSteps.title: Test the tank sensor
fixSteps.instruction: The water tank typically has a magnet and the machine has a reed switch for detection. Verify the magnet is present and intact in the tank. If the reed switch in the machine is faulty, it requires professional repair.

whenToCallPro: If the tank is full and properly seated but E01 persists, the internal sensor may need professional replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Breville espresso E01 water tank error fix

faq.q: What does E01 mean on a Breville?
faq.a: E01 means the water tank is empty, missing, or not detected. Refill and reseat the tank.

faq.q: Why does E01 appear when the tank is full?
faq.a: The tank may not be seated properly on the valve, or the tank sensor may have debris preventing detection.

faq.q: Can a clogged water filter cause E01?
faq.a: A severely clogged filter can restrict flow enough to trigger the error. Try removing the filter to test.

title: Thermocoil or Boiler Temperature Fault
shortDescription: The heating system is not reaching or maintaining the correct brew temperature.
fullDescription: Error E02 indicates the thermocoil or boiler temperature is outside the expected operating range. The machine may be overheating or underheating. This can be caused by a faulty temperature sensor, failed heating element, tripped thermal fuse, or heavy mineral buildup insulating the heater.

causes.cause: Heavy mineral scale insulating the thermocoil reducing heat transfer
Faulty NTC temperature sensor providing wrong readings
Tripped thermal fuse or thermostat from overheating event
Failed heating element no longer producing heat

symptoms: E02 error on display
Machine takes much longer to heat up than normal
Brew water is too cold or too hot
Steam weak or nonexistent
Machine shuts down during the heating phase

fixSteps.title: Run a descale cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Mineral buildup is the most common cause of temperature faults. Perform a complete descaling cycle using Breville descaler to remove deposits from the thermocoil.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the machine
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the machine and unplug it for 30 minutes. This allows thermal fuses to reset if they have tripped due to overheating. Plug back in and test.

fixSteps.title: Check ventilation
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the machine has adequate ventilation around it. Machines pushed against walls or under cabinets can overheat. Allow at least 10cm clearance on all sides.

fixSteps.title: Professional temperature system diagnosis
fixSteps.instruction: If descaling and power cycling do not resolve E02, the NTC sensor, thermal fuse, or heating element may need testing and replacement by a qualified technician.

whenToCallPro: If descaling does not resolve E02, internal heating components need professional testing and repair.
videoSearchQuery: Breville espresso E02 temperature error fix

faq.q: What does E02 mean?
faq.a: E02 indicates a heating system temperature fault. Descaling is the first thing to try as scale insulation is the most common cause.

faq.q: Can scale buildup cause E02?
faq.a: Yes. Scale insulates the thermocoil, making it harder to regulate temperature accurately. Descaling often resolves this.

faq.q: Is E02 dangerous?
faq.a: The machine shuts down to prevent overheating, so it is safe. However, the underlying issue should be resolved before continuing to use the machine.

title: Pump Pressure Fault
shortDescription: The pump is unable to generate adequate brewing pressure for espresso extraction.
fullDescription: Error E03 indicates the brew pump cannot reach the required pressure for proper espresso extraction. Normal brew pressure is approximately 9 bars. This error can be caused by a worn pump, air in the system, or a scale-clogged system restricting the pump's ability to pressurize.

causes.cause: Air trapped in the water system after tank removal
Worn or failing vibratory pump (ULKA)
Severely clogged system from mineral scale
Blocked or damaged solenoid valve

symptoms: E03 error on display
Very weak or thin espresso flow
Pump sounds louder or strained than normal
No pressure showing on the gauge (if equipped)
Coffee puck is soupy and wet after extraction

fixSteps.title: Prime the pump
fixSteps.instruction: Fill the water tank. Run the hot water/steam function briefly to purge air from the system. Then run a brew cycle without coffee to flush the water path.

fixSteps.title: Check grind setting
fixSteps.instruction: If the grind is too fine, it can choke the system and trigger a pressure fault. Adjust to a coarser grind setting and try again.

fixSteps.title: Descale the machine
fixSteps.instruction: Run a full descaling cycle. Scale deposits in the pump and solenoid valve can severely restrict flow and pressure buildup.

fixSteps.title: Professional pump inspection
fixSteps.instruction: If E03 persists after priming, adjusting grind, and descaling, the vibratory pump or solenoid valve may be worn and need replacement.

whenToCallPro: If priming and descaling do not resolve the pressure fault, the pump or solenoid likely needs professional replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Breville espresso E03 pump pressure error fix

faq.q: What does E03 mean?
faq.a: E03 means the pump cannot generate adequate pressure for espresso extraction. Try priming the system and descaling first.

faq.q: Can air in the system cause E03?
faq.a: Yes. Air pockets after refilling the water tank can prevent the pump from building pressure. Running hot water briefly usually purges the air.

faq.q: How long does a Breville pump last?
faq.a: ULKA vibratory pumps typically last 5-8 years with normal home use. Heavy use or hard water can shorten this.

title: Steam Boiler Fault
shortDescription: The steam boiler is not heating properly or has exceeded safe temperature limits.
fullDescription: Error E04 on dual-boiler Breville machines indicates a fault with the steam boiler specifically. The boiler may not be heating to steam temperature, may be overheating, or the temperature sensor may be providing erroneous readings. This affects steam and hot water functions but the brew boiler may still operate.

causes.cause: Scale buildup in the steam boiler reducing heating efficiency
Faulty steam boiler temperature sensor
Steam boiler heating element failure
Thermal fuse tripped on steam boiler circuit

symptoms: E04 error on display
No steam produced from steam wand
Steam takes very long to become ready
Hot water function not working
Machine shows steam ready indicator but produces only lukewarm water

fixSteps.title: Descale the machine
fixSteps.instruction: Run a complete descale cycle. Steam boilers are particularly susceptible to scale buildup because water is heated to higher temperatures, accelerating mineral deposition.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle with extended rest
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off and unplug the machine for 30 minutes. This allows any tripped thermal protection to reset. Plug back in and test the steam function.

fixSteps.title: Flush the steam boiler
fixSteps.instruction: Open the steam wand fully and dispense hot water for 30-60 seconds to flush the steam boiler of any trapped air or debris.

fixSteps.title: Professional steam boiler repair
fixSteps.instruction: If E04 persists, the steam boiler heating element, temperature sensor, or thermal fuse needs professional testing and replacement.

whenToCallPro: If descaling and power cycling do not resolve E04, internal steam boiler components need professional service.
videoSearchQuery: Breville dual boiler E04 steam boiler error fix

faq.q: What does E04 mean?
faq.a: E04 indicates a steam boiler heating fault, typically found on dual-boiler Breville models.

faq.q: Does E04 affect espresso brewing?
faq.a: E04 typically only affects steam and hot water functions. The brew boiler may still work normally.

faq.q: Can I fix E04 myself?
faq.a: Try descaling first, as it is the most common cause. If that does not work, steam boiler repair generally requires a technician.

title: Flow Meter Error
shortDescription: The flow meter is not detecting water flow or is reading incorrectly.
fullDescription: Error E05 signals that the internal flow meter, which measures water volume during brewing, is providing no signal or erratic readings. The flow meter is essential for volumetric dosing on machines with programmed shot volumes.

causes.cause: Air bubble trapped in the flow meter impeller area
Scale buildup blocking the flow meter impeller
Faulty flow meter sensor or hall effect switch
Loose wiring connection to flow meter

symptoms: E05 error on display
Shots run indefinitely without auto-stopping
Shot volumes wildly inconsistent
Machine does not start brew despite pressing button
Programmed shot sizes produce wrong amounts

fixSteps.title: Purge air from the system
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and refill the water tank. Run several blank shots (no coffee) through the group head to purge air from the system and the flow meter.

fixSteps.title: Descale the machine
fixSteps.instruction: Scale deposits can block the small impeller inside the flow meter. Run a complete descaling cycle.

fixSteps.title: Reset the machine
fixSteps.instruction: Perform a factory reset using the procedure in your model's manual. This resets flow meter calibration data and may resolve software-related issues.

fixSteps.title: Professional flow meter replacement
fixSteps.instruction: If E05 persists, the flow meter needs replacement. This is an internal component requiring disassembly of the machine by a technician.

whenToCallPro: If purging and descaling do not fix inconsistent shot volumes, the flow meter likely needs replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Breville espresso E05 flow meter error fix

faq.q: What does E05 mean?
faq.a: E05 means the flow meter that measures water volume during brewing is not working correctly.

faq.q: Can I use the machine in manual mode with E05?
faq.a: You may be able to brew manually by pressing and holding the brew button, but automatic volume dosing will not work.

faq.q: What is a flow meter?
faq.a: A flow meter is a small device with an impeller that spins as water passes through, counting the volume of water delivered during a shot.

title: Control Board Communication Error
shortDescription: Internal communication failure between the control board and sub-systems.
fullDescription: Error E06 indicates a communication breakdown between the main control board and one of the machine's sub-systems (grinder, display, pump controller). This can be caused by a loose internal connector, power surge damage, or a failing control board.

causes.cause: Loose internal ribbon cable or wiring connector
Power surge damage to control board circuits
Faulty main control board
Moisture or steam damage to internal electronics

symptoms: E06 error on display
Machine completely unresponsive to all buttons
Display flickers or shows garbled text
Machine powers on then immediately shows error
Random shutdowns during operation

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the machine
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off and unplug the machine for 10 minutes. Plug back in and power on. Temporary communication glitches often resolve with a full power cycle.

fixSteps.title: Check for steam or moisture damage
fixSteps.instruction: If the machine has been steaming excessively or was used in a very humid environment, let it dry for 24 hours with the tank removed and panels open if possible.

fixSteps.title: Inspect internal connectors
fixSteps.instruction: If you are comfortable opening the machine, remove the top cover screws on the back. Check all ribbon cables and connectors are firmly seated. Reseat any loose connections.

fixSteps.title: Professional board repair
fixSteps.instruction: If E06 persists, the main control board likely needs testing or replacement. This should be done by an authorized Breville service center.

whenToCallPro: If a power cycle does not clear E06, the machine should be inspected by an authorized service center.
videoSearchQuery: Breville espresso E06 control board error fix

faq.q: What does E06 mean?
faq.a: E06 means there is a communication failure between the machine's control board and its subsystems. Try a power cycle first.

faq.q: Is E06 a serious error?
faq.a: It can be. While sometimes resolved by a power cycle, persistent E06 errors typically indicate a control board issue requiring service.

faq.q: Does Breville warranty cover E06?
faq.a: If within the warranty period (typically 2 years), contact Breville for warranty repair. E06 from normal use should be covered.

title: Grinder Jammed or Blocked
shortDescription: The built-in grinder is jammed and cannot turn or has encountered an obstruction.
fullDescription: The grinder jam error occurs on Breville machines with integrated grinders when the grinder motor cannot rotate the burrs. This is typically caused by a foreign object (stone, twig) in the coffee beans, excessively oily beans clogging the burrs, or worn burrs that have seized.

causes.cause: Foreign object (stone, twig) in the bean hopper
Oily or flavored beans clogging the burrs and grind chute
Stale grounds packed around the burrs from previous use
Worn or chipped conical burr set

symptoms: Grinder error message or grinder icon warning on display
Grinding noise stops suddenly or sounds strained
No ground coffee produced in the portafilter
Grinder motor buzzes but burrs do not turn
Inconsistent or very coarse grind output

fixSteps.title: Empty the bean hopper
fixSteps.instruction: Remove remaining beans from the hopper. Look carefully for any foreign objects like small stones or twigs mixed in with the beans. Use a vacuum to clean the hopper.

fixSteps.title: Remove and clean the upper burr
fixSteps.instruction: Set the grind to the coarsest setting. Remove the hopper and twist to unlock the upper burr. Use the included brush to clean ground coffee from around both burrs, the chute, and the grinding chamber.

fixSteps.title: Run grinder cleaning tablets
fixSteps.instruction: Place Breville grinder cleaning tablets in the hopper. Run the grinder — the tablets absorb oils and push out stale grounds from the burrs and chute.

fixSteps.title: Inspect and replace burrs
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect both burrs for chips, cracks, or excessive wear. Breville recommends replacing conical burrs every 2-3 years for home use. Install new burrs if worn.

whenToCallPro: If the grinder motor does not turn even with clean burrs and no obstructions, the motor may be burned out and needs professional repair.
videoSearchQuery: Breville Barista Express grinder jammed fix cleaning

faq.q: Why is my Breville grinder jammed?
faq.a: The most common cause is a foreign object (stone) in the beans or oily residue clogging the burrs. Clean the grinder thoroughly.

faq.q: Can oily beans damage the grinder?
faq.a: Very oily beans can clog the grinder chute and burrs over time. Use medium-roast beans and clean the grinder regularly.

faq.q: How often should I clean the grinder?
faq.a: Clean the grinder with a brush every 2 weeks and run cleaning tablets monthly for best performance.

title: Insufficient Extraction Pressure
shortDescription: The pressure gauge shows low pressure during extraction, resulting in weak or watery espresso.
fullDescription: The pressure gauge reads below the ideal 9-12 bar range during extraction. Low pressure results in under-extracted espresso that is sour, watery, and lacks crema. This is most commonly a user technique issue (grind, dose, tamp) rather than a machine fault.

causes.cause: Grind too coarse for espresso extraction
Insufficient coffee dose in the basket
Tamp pressure too light or uneven
Stale beans that have lost CO2

symptoms: Pressure gauge needle below the green espresso zone
Espresso runs too fast (under 20 seconds for a double)
Little to no crema on the espresso
Watery, sour-tasting espresso
Shot gushes out of the portafilter

fixSteps.title: Adjust the grind finer
fixSteps.instruction: Turn the grind size dial one or two notches finer. The espresso should take 25-30 seconds to extract a double shot of approximately 36ml.

fixSteps.title: Increase the dose
fixSteps.instruction: Use 18-20 grams of coffee for a double basket. Weigh the dose with a kitchen scale for consistency. Use the Razor tool to trim excess if needed.

fixSteps.title: Tamp with consistent pressure
fixSteps.instruction: Tamp level and firm with approximately 30 pounds of pressure. The puck surface should be smooth, level, and even.

fixSteps.title: Check for channeling
fixSteps.instruction: After extraction, inspect the puck. If there are holes or uneven saturation, water is channeling through weak spots. Distribute grounds more evenly before tamping using a distribution tool.

whenToCallPro: If low pressure persists with correct grind, dose, and tamp, the pump may need service.
videoSearchQuery: Breville espresso low pressure no crema fix grind

faq.q: What pressure should the gauge show?
faq.a: The needle should be in the green espresso range, approximately 9-12 bar during extraction.

faq.q: Does grind size really matter that much?
faq.a: Yes, grind size is the single most important variable in espresso extraction. Small adjustments make a big difference.

faq.q: Why does my espresso have no crema?
faq.a: No crema indicates under-extraction. Common causes are grind too coarse, dose too low, stale beans, or insufficient tamp pressure.
